text.title: pytest-django Setup and Markers
text.summary: pyproject.toml config, conftest.py structure, @pytest.mark.django_db variants, and --reuse-db for faster iteration.

rule.statement: Configure pytest-django via `pyproject.toml` `[tool.pytest.ini_options]`. Set `DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE`, `addopts` with `--strict-markers`, and `testpaths = ["tests"]`.
rule.rationale: Centralizing pytest config in `pyproject.toml` eliminates `pytest.ini` / `setup.cfg` duplication. `--strict-markers` fails on unknown markers, catching typos in `@pytest.mark.slow` or custom marks. `--strict-markers` requires all marks to be registered under `markers`.

example.code: [tool.pytest.ini_options] DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "config.settings.testing" python_files = ["test_*.py"] addopts = "-v --tb=short --strict-markers --reuse-db" testpaths = ["tests"] markers = [ "slow: marks tests that hit external services", "integration: end-to-end integration tests", ]

rule.statement: Use `@pytest.mark.django_db` (savepoint rollback) for all model and service tests by default. Use `@pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)` only for code that calls `on_commit` or uses raw transactions.
rule.rationale: The default `django_db` marker wraps each test in a savepoint and rolls back after — zero DB state leaks, fast execution. `transaction=True` commits real transactions but must truncate tables after each test, making it 5-10x slower. It is only needed for `post_save`/`on_commit` signals or Celery tasks that check committed state.

rule.statement: Use `--reuse-db` in local development to skip recreating the test database on every run. Use `--create-db` to force recreation after schema changes.
rule.rationale: `--reuse-db` skips the `migrate` step when the schema has not changed, reducing test suite startup from 10-30s to under 1s on large projects. It is safe for all tests that use `@pytest.mark.django_db` (rollback isolation). Use `--create-db` after any migration change.

section.title: conftest.py Structure

rule.statement: Put shared fixtures in `tests/conftest.py`: `api_client`, `authed_client`, `staff_client`, and factory registrations. Keep conftest thin — move test-specific fixtures close to the tests that use them.
rule.rationale: A thin conftest with only shared infrastructure (client fixtures, authed helpers, factory registrations) loads fast and remains readable. Test-specific fixtures pollute conftest and increase fixture discovery time as the test suite grows.

section.reference: Shared conftest.py: api_client, authed_client, factory registration
